column2: Encode "validators-cannot-self-validate-their-own-fix-prs" as a Tier-2 AMS constraint
column3: The pattern of fix-the-validator → ship-blind → validate-on-next-PR has recurred at least five times across distinct PRs in this project, most recently PR #87 (2026-05-13) where the streaming SSE fix to worker/src/runtime/audit-gate.ts could not be exercised by PR #87's own runtime probes because those probes call https://ams.klappy.dev/audit-gate-test which runs whatever code is currently on main. PR #87 merged with the gap unsurfaced in the PR description until the operator named it directly in chat at merge time. Canonized as ams://canon/constraints/validators-cannot-self-validate-their-own-fix-prs with status: active, stability: semi_stable. Derives_from klappy://canon/methods/governance-validation-via-agents, klappy://canon/principles/verification-requires-fresh-context, ams://canon/constraints/outcome-verification-via-runnable-artifact. The constraint names the structural cause (production-bound validators run against main, not against PR branch code), prescribes the minimum honesty discipline (PR description must name the validation gap for any production-bound-validator-modifying PR), and surfaces optional better mitigations (staging URL override, local logic tests, canary deploy with rollback) without prescribing which to adopt.

column2: This PR is also the first qualifying PR after PR #87 merged — the real parity event for the streaming dispatcher
column3: The first PR opened after PR #87 merged (which auto-deployed the streaming SSE worker code to production via Cloudflare Workers Git integration) is this one. By the constraint encoded above, this PR's runtime probes should now exercise the streaming dispatcher against api.anthropic.com in a way the non-streaming dispatcher could not. Expected verdict matrix: legacy canon-code-sync-audit.yml gates with PASS; canon-code-sync-audit-runtime-probe completes with a real verdict comment (not anthropic_api_failed: 524); oddkit-gauntlet-runtime-probe completes with a real gauntlet verdict on the new constraint doc plus this journal entry; output-artifact-validation-runtime-probe completes with a real verdict on the new journal TSV in scope. If 524 returns on any probe, the streaming switch did not fix the root cause and a different investigation is required.

column2: Production-bound-validator-fix PRs must name the validation gap in their PR description
column3: Per the new constraint, this PR's description includes a "Validation Gap Disclosure" section naming: which validator (if any) is being modified, why the PR cannot exercise the fix in itself, what the gates on this PR run against, when the fix actually validates, and what success looks like on the validation-event PR. For this PR specifically: no production-bound validator is being modified — the changes are canon authoring plus a journal entry. The disclosure on this PR is therefore "N/A; first qualifying PR after PR #87's streaming dispatcher deployed, observing real probe verdicts is the goal." Future PRs that DO modify a production-bound validator will fill out the full disclosure.

column2: Constraint scope is bounded — does not prescribe which architectural mitigation to adopt
column3: The constraint enumerates three mitigations (staging URL via workflow input override, local logic tests in CI, canary deploy with rollback) but explicitly does not prescribe one. The right choice is per-validator and depends on the cost of false negatives. AMS currently uses the canary pattern implicitly; the new constraint just names the window honestly so the operator sees it at merge time instead of after a frustrated cycle.

column2: Cursor agent landed a follow-up SSE buffer flush fix on PR #87 before merge
column3: Between my initial streaming commit (16eb30b) and the merge of PR #87, a Cursor agent landed commit a68607b "fix(audit-gate): flush trailing SSE buffer on stream end" — 14 lines added, 2 removed in worker/src/runtime/audit-gate.ts. The fix addresses an edge case in my streaming parser where a final SSE event without a terminating blank line would not be flushed before the stream closes. Second pair of eyes caught the bug; the streaming dispatcher that auto-deployed to production includes both commits. Worth noting for canon's "verification-requires-fresh-context" principle in action: a different agent in a different session caught what the authoring session missed.

column2: Post-merge: future PRs modifying production-bound validators inherit the new constraint
column3: Post-merge of this PR: ams://canon/constraints/validators-cannot-self-validate-their-own-fix-prs is active canon. Any future PR that modifies any production-bound validator (audit gates, runtime probes, deploy-time checks, scheduled drift detectors, or any new validator added later) must name the validation gap in its PR description per the constraint's honesty discipline section. The constraint's existence does not block such PRs; the gates do not enforce the disclosure (yet — a future enhancement could have the canon-code-sync gate scan PR descriptions for the disclosure when relevant files are modified). Compliance is by operator and authoring-agent attention to the discipline. Standing flags unchanged: PAT and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in project instructions remain unrotated.
